verses.text: Shall we sin that grace may abound? 'God forbid'; how shall we, dead to sin, live therein? No license. Grace frees from sin, not to it.
Shall we sin that grace may abound? 'God forbid'; how shall we, dead to sin, live therein? No license. Grace frees from sin, not to it.
Baptized into His death, buried with Him, that we 'walk in newness of life.' New life. Union with Christ means a changed walk.
Baptized into His death, buried with Him, that we 'walk in newness of life.' New life. Union with Christ means a changed walk.
Baptized into His death, buried with Him, that we 'walk in newness of life.' New life. Union with Christ means a changed walk.
Our 'old man is crucified,' that the body of sin be destroyed; the dead is freed from sin. Old self slain. Sin's reign is broken in Christ.
Our 'old man is crucified,' that the body of sin be destroyed; the dead is freed from sin. Old self slain. Sin's reign is broken in Christ.
Christ, raised, dies no more; 'reckon ye also yourselves to be dead indeed unto sin, but alive unto God.' Reckon it so. Faith counts on union with the risen Christ.
Christ, raised, dies no more; 'reckon ye also yourselves to be dead indeed unto sin, but alive unto God.' Reckon it so. Faith counts on union with the risen Christ.
Christ, raised, dies no more; 'reckon ye also yourselves to be dead indeed unto sin, but alive unto God.' Reckon it so. Faith counts on union with the risen Christ.
Christ, raised, dies no more; 'reckon ye also yourselves to be dead indeed unto sin, but alive unto God.' Reckon it so. Faith counts on union with the risen Christ.
Let not sin reign; yield yourselves to God; 'sin shall not have dominion over you.' Freedom. Grace, not law, breaks sin's rule.
Let not sin reign; yield yourselves to God; 'sin shall not have dominion over you.' Freedom. Grace, not law, breaks sin's rule.
Let not sin reign; yield yourselves to God; 'sin shall not have dominion over you.' Freedom. Grace, not law, breaks sin's rule.
Made free from sin, 'ye became the servants of righteousness.' New master. To leave sin is to serve righteousness.
Made free from sin, 'ye became the servants of righteousness.' New master. To leave sin is to serve righteousness.
Made free from sin, 'ye became the servants of righteousness.' New master. To leave sin is to serve righteousness.
Made free from sin, 'ye became the servants of righteousness.' New master. To leave sin is to serve righteousness.
Once servants of sin, free from righteousness; now yield your members 'servants to righteousness unto holiness.' Changed service. The members once given to sin now serve God.
Once servants of sin, free from righteousness; now yield your members 'servants to righteousness unto holiness.' Changed service. The members once given to sin now serve God.
Sin's end is death; 'the wages of sin is death; but the gift of God is eternal life through Jesus Christ.' Wages and gift. Sin earns death; God gives life freely.
